impact:
CONFIRMED VALID, but ONLY MEASURABLE AT BLEND=1.00 — and BLOCKED BY ISS-008 for any other setting.

Original: P1's main fix (C15=8.2n, C17=1.8n) brought 8k/10k within ±1.5dB (was -3.4). Residual at 12.5k/16k (-5.9/-19.1dB) is cumulative bilinear warp of the V2 recovery LPF cascade, not a component value. Ruled out: C16 470p->330p (overshot +4.7dB at 8k), C14 47n->39n (regressed LF hump to 2.04dB), C32/C29 22p->15p (zero effect, confirms error is pre-tone-stack), C42 10n->12n (worse). Likely needs prewarp (utils/Prewarp.h exists, unused) or extending the oversampled region per dsp.md "Top-octave accuracy".

2026-07-16 corroboration + blocker: the BL=1.00 captures reproduce this diagnosis closely (12.9kHz -5.2/-7.3/-1.3dB, 16.3kHz -25.1/-13.9/-18.1dB across the three V2 BL=1.00 captures) — so the wet-path top-octave deficit is real and the bilinear-warp explanation stands.

BUT at BL<1.00 the sign INVERTS to a massive EXCESS (12.9kHz: +24.5 @BL=0.95, +26.5 @BL=0.90, +54.4 @BL=0.50) because V2's dry tap is grossly too hot (ISS-008). The two errors are opposite in sign, so the dry excess MASKS this deficit at any partial blend. Any attempt to fit prewarp/oversampling against a BL<1.00 capture will be fitting against the masked sum and will produce a wrong answer.

Fix ISS-008 first, or restrict all measurement for this issue to the three BL=1.00 captures. Reproduce: python3.11 analysis/cascade_analysis.py (section C).
title: V2 12.5k/16k HF residual is cumulative bilinear warp
